Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiff Andrew Sisneros To Enforce Judgment And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brian Tagawa, Esq. 415-951-8600 for PLAINTIFF/PETITIONER ANDREW SISNEROS.
Boris Reznikov, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-4296 for Defendant/Respondent San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Hearing is held. the Court announces the Hearing is for the sole purpose of the court issuing a ruling on the motion. No further argument is permitted.
The Court having reviewed the pleadings rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANDREW SISNEROS' Motion To Enforce Judgment And For Sanctions. The Court issues it's rulings as stated on the Court Reporter's trancript.

Defendant's Counsel to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiff Andrew Sisneros To Enforce Judgment And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-30-2018 Continued To Feb-13-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Barry K. Tagawa, Esq. of the Law Office of Barry K. Tagawa, T:415.951.8600, for petitioner/moving party;
Boris Reznikov, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, T:415.554.3800, for respondent CCSF.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, PETITIONER ANDREW SISNEROS' Motion To Enforce Judgment And For Sanctions is continued to February 13, 2018 at 9:30 am for the Court to issue its ruling. The request for sanctions is denied for reasons stated on the record. The remainder of the motion is taken under submission.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiff Andrew Sisneros To Enforce Judgment And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-25-2018 Continued To Jan-30-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Barry Tagawa Esq. 415-951-8600 for Petitioner Andrew Sisneros who is also present.
Boris Reznikov, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3800 of the Office of the City Attorney for Respondent CCSF.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court shares his views on the issues of calculation of pay, pre-judgment interest, post-judgment interest and sanctions. Mr. Sisneros is sworn and examined. Both sides Argue. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER ANDREW SISNEROS' Motion To Enforce Judgment And For Sanctions is continued to January 30, 2018 at 9:30 am to allow parties to meet and confer re: "block" pay. Respondent to retrieve information on "block" pay and to provide a copy of information to Petitioner's Counsel by tomorrow. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, mtorreano@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

Motion For Attorneys Fees Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1021.5
Off Calendar Aug-17-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BRYAN SCHWARTZ, TEL. NO. 1-510-944-9300 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. BORRS REZNIKOV, CITY ATTORNEY'S OFFICE, TEL. NO. 1-415-554-4296 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER ANDREW SISNEROS' Motion For Attorneys Fees Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1021.5 is DENIED. Code of Civil Procedure section 1021.5 exists "to provide some incentive for the plaintiff who acts as a true private attorney general, prosecuting a lawsuit that enforces an important public right and confers a significant benefit, despite the fact that his or her own financial stake in the outcome would not by itself constitute an adequate incentive to litigate." (Satrap v. Pacific Gas & Elec. Co. (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 72, 76.) Petitioner fails to carry his burden to show that his lawsuit vindicated an important public interest or conferred a significant benefit on society or a large class of persons. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
